mnemonics: Open Covers Need Diamonds (OCND): To remember that an Open Cover of a set is a collection of open sets whose union contains the set, and its subcover is a finite selection from that collection.
Every Cauchy Is Really Elegant (E.C.I.R.E): A mnemonic for 'Every Cauchy sequence Is Really Elegant' to recall that in a complete metric space, every Cauchy sequence converges.
keyPoints: A metric space is a set X with a distance function d satisfying positivity, symmetry, and the triangle inequality.
Key concepts include open/closed sets, convergence, completeness, compactness (characterized by sequential compactness or being totally bounded and complete), and connectedness.
The Bolzano-Weierstrass property in R^n generalizes to sequential compactness in metric spaces.
commonMistakes: Confusing 'open ball' with 'open set'; an open ball is an example, but an open set is any set that contains an open ball around each of its points.
Assuming a set is closed because its complement is not open; a set can be both open and closed (clopen) or neither.
Believing that convergence implies Cauchy without checking the space's completeness; this is only true in complete metric spaces.
